data_type: WKWebView Session Data (URLs, cookies, cache, local storage)
description: WKWebView loads HA frontend dashboard. Cookies, local storage, and cache persisted via WKWebsiteDataStore. User-agent includes app name/version.

flow_steps.details: WebViewController initialized with server context; loads HA frontend URL.
WKUserContentController configured with message handlers: getExternalAuth, revokeExternalAuth, externalBus, updateThemeColors, logError.
WKWebView configured with app-specific user agent and content mode.

flow_steps.code: static func makeWebViewConfiguration() -> WKWebViewConfiguration { config.allowsInlineMediaPlayback = true }
flow_steps.details: WebView configuration allows inline media playback and sets media types requiring user action.
WKWebsiteDataStore manages cookies, disk cache, memory cache, fetch cache, and service worker registrations.

flow_steps.code: func setupURLObserver() { urlObserver = webView.observe(\.url) { ... userActivity?.webpageURL = cleanURL } }
flow_steps.details: Current WebView URL observed and set on NSUserActivity for Handoff.

description: JavaScript bridge communicates between HA frontend and native app: external auth tokens, Haptics, Assist, and notifications.

flow_steps.details: Handles script messages from frontend JavaScript: getExternalAuth provides bearer token to web content, revokeExternalAuth clears session.
External message bus handles haptic feedback, assist requests, and Improv BLE scanning commands from frontend.

description: WebView URL is shared via NSUserActivity for Handoff between Apple devices. Associated domains declared for universal links.

flow_steps.details: Current frontend URL shared via NSUserActivity for cross-device Handoff.
Associated domains declared for universal links to home-assistant.io.

description: App declares NSCrossWebsiteTrackingUsageDescription for optionally enabling cross-site tracking in WKWebView if user configuration requires it.

flow_steps.code: <key>NSCrossWebsiteTrackingUsageDescription</key><string>Optionally enable cross-website tracking if your configuration requires it.</string>
flow_steps.details: Cross-website tracking declared in Info.plist for optional WKWebView tracking configuration.

description: Pull-to-refresh clears frontend asset cache (disk cache, memory cache, fetch cache, service worker registrations) then reloads.

flow_steps.details: User pulls to refresh; frontend asset cache is cleaned before reload.
